Pavel heads north to Ballard; overcast but dry Saturday makes farmers market appealing. Budget 50 min for cross-town drive from South Lake Union.

Pavel gets 7.5 hours of restorative sleep after a full, intentional Saturday. Sleep beat spans overnight as required.

diary:
Woke up early with Milo, my usual ritual—coffee by the window, some journaling before the city fully woke up. There's something about those quiet moments in South Lake Union that grounds me. Took him for a walk, then headed to Ballard for the farmers market. The overcast sky felt fitting somehow; I wasn't in a rush. Chatted with a vendor about heirloom tomatoes, picked up some fresh herbs. The market has this energy even on a gray Saturday.

Back home for brunch, then I dragged myself out to Discovery Park for a long walk. Saw a couple of herons by the water—reminded me why I needed the air. Spent the afternoon with some poetry, just letting thoughts wander. The best part was meeting my friend at Café Solstice in Capitol Hill. We talked for an hour, the kind of conversation that makes you feel less alone. Came home, made dinner, and now Milo's asleep on the couch while I wind down. Feels like a day that actually belonged to me.
thought_process: I needed this Saturday to be mine—not cramped into work mode or obligations. The overcast weather actually helped; it gave me permission to move slowly. I skipped the Mariners crowds deliberately; too many people on a day like this would've drained me. The farmers market was social but manageable, then I retreated. Discovery Park cleared my head in a way nothing else does. By afternoon, I was ready for genuine connection, so the coffee with my friend felt perfectly timed. At fifty-eight, I'm learning that these paced days matter more than packed ones. My mood started contemplative and stayed that way—no jarring shifts, just a gradual settling into contentment.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
